title: Tetan carbonite smelter
content:
{{Top|leg|canon=Tetan refinery}} {{SpaceStationClass |image=[[File:Miningoutpost.jpg]] |name=Tetan carbonite smelter |designer= |manufacturer= |line= |type=[[Space station/Legends|Space station]][[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith'' audio drama]] |cost= |length= |width= |height= |diameter= |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed= |maneuverability= |engine= |hyperdrive= |hdrange= |hdsystem= |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen= |hull= |sensor= |target= |navigation= |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures=[[Laser cannon/Legends|Laser cannons]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 1]] |armament= |complement= |bays=Present[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 1|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 1]] |escapepods= |crew= |skeleton= |passengers= |capacity= |cargohandling= |consumables= |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |availability= |role=[[Carbonite/Legends|Carbonite]] smelter |firstuse= |retired= |affiliation=*[[Carbonite Guild]] *[[Krath/Legends|Krath]] }} {{Quote|Our…uh…willing cohort ''General Vulpe'' informs me that the ''last'' of the company smelters has ''surrendered'' to our rule.|Aleema Keto|}} The '''Tetan carbonite smelters''' in the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]] processed [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]], making the [[Carbonite Guild]] and [[Keto family/Legends|Keto family]] very wealthy. During the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3997 BBY]], the [[Space station/Legends|space stations]] were captured by the [[Krath/Legends|Krath]] during the cult's [[Political uprising of the Empress Teta system|successful coup]] of the system. One carbonite smelter was being inspected by [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Keto]] and his sister [[Magda Keto|Magda]] the leaders of the Empress Teta system when [[Satal Keto/Legends|Satal]] and [[Aleema Keto]] of the Krath came to the station and [[Death/Legends|killed]] the Tetan rulers by encasing them in molten carbonite. The next year, another station was [[Attack on a Tetan carbonite smelter|attacked]] by the [[Mandalorian Crusaders]] who deployed an [[atomic compression bomb]] against the [[Realspace/Legends|space]] station, which caused an explosion on the station and sent it crashing down to the planet's surface. ==Description== {{Quote|As you can see, my Lord Keto, the carbonite operation is running quite smoothly.''"
"''Indeed, Chairman Bearus. You've improved this space station's profit margins by fifteen percent!|Bearus and Lord Keto|}} A type of [[Space station/Legends|space station]], the Tetan carbonite smelters were several space stations that were used by the [[Carbonite Guild]] for smelting the [[Ore/Legends|ore]] of the metal [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]]. The space stations varied in color, with one being a [[Color/Legends|red]]-colored and another being black in color. The carbonite smelters were metal structures that had numerous antennae on their top and bottom surfaces. Two of the [[Tetan/Legends|Tetan]] [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]] smelters the station had at least four intake vents through which [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]] rich with the [[Ore/Legends|ore]] of the metal carbonite were drawn into the smelter. The ore was subsequently smelted in large vats within the station in order to obtain the carbonite.The carbonite smelters were equipped with [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] that were used to shoot at the larger asteroids and split them into chunks of smaller size. The space stations had spacious interiors with a constant traffic of [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]]-equipped vehicles within as well as [[Hangar/Legends|docking facilities]] that could hold at least two [[Unidentified Tetan royal ship|Tetan royal starships]]. The Tetan carbonite smelter had a crew of at least three carbonite workers, could support at least sixteen passengers and had a [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|security force]] aboard. ==History== {{Dialogue|Carbonite smelter worker|Chairman Bearus! Another royal ship is approaching the station, carrying the royal tutor Korus and the lord's children Satal and Aleema.|Keto|My son?! And niece?!|Bearus|They weren't on the list?|Carbonite smelter worker|It's an unscheduled trip, my lord. Should I send them away?|attr=Lord Keto and Bearus learn of the arrival of Satal and Aleema|Bearus|No, no, let them join us. I'm sure Lord Keto won't mind|src=}} [[File:Tetan_carbonite_smelter_under_attack.jpg|thumb|left|150px|A smelter under Mandalorian attack]] The Tetan carbonite smelters were used by the Carbonite Guild for smelting the ore of the metal carbonite by the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3997 BBY]].Page 129 of ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' dates the [[Political uprising of the Empress Teta system|Krath takeover]] of the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]] to the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3997 BBY]]. [[Bearus]], the [[Chairman/Legends|Chairman]] of the Guild, had managed to improved the one smelter's profit margins by fifteen percent by 3997 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]]. That year, [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Keto]]—the head of the [[Tetan monarchy/Legends|Tetan Monarchy]]—and [[Gender/Legends|his]] sister [[Magda Keto|Magda]] along with their royal entourage visited the carbonite smelter on an annual inspection tour. The royals were welcomed aboard the station by Bearus himself. After exchanging pleasantries, the group was notified of the unexpected and imminent arrival of the royal tutor [[Korus]] and his group of six students, which included the royal heirs [[Satal Keto/Legends|Satal]] and [[Aleema Keto]] and whose members were part of the [[Krath/Legends|Krath]], a secret [[Sith cult/Legends|Sith cult]]. Bearus and the royals commenced the tour with the former pointing out to the latter the working efficiency of the carbonite smelters. In the meantime, Korus and the students approached the group and were immediately confronted by Lord Keto, who reproached Korus for bringing the students to the carbonite smelter during the royal inspection and thus violating protocol. As a response to Keto's accusations, Korus fell into a comatose state as a victim of an [[Mind trick/Legends|Force illusion]] cast on him by Aleema, who had been practicing [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith sorcery]]. That horrified the royals and caused Bearus to try to call the security force. However, with the help of illusions, Aleema was able to both halt Bearus's attempt and send the advancing Tetan royal guards into a catatonic shock. As a next step of their [[Political uprising of the Empress Teta system|political uprising]], the Krath members dragged the royals toward the molten carbonite vats. After a carbonite worker that was attacked by one of Aleema's illusions stumbled off the ledge into the vat, the Krath proceeded to slowly lower Lord Keto and his sister into the carbonite, [[Death/Legends|killing]] them and encasing them within the metal. Following the successful coup, the Krath eventually took over all of the [[Empress/Legends|Empress]] [[Teta/Legends|Teta]] [[Star system/Legends|system]], including all the Tetan carbonite smelters, and hung the metal cases containing the remains of Lord Keto and his sister on the wall of the Krath [[Iron Citadel/Legends|citadel]] for display. During the Krath takeover of the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]], all of the Tetan carbonite smelters were captured by the Krath. Shortly before the Krath celebration of their takeover's success, the Krath [[General/Legends|General]] [[Vulpe]] informed one of the cult's leaders, Aleema Keto, of the last Carbonite Guild–owned carbonite smelter's surrender. Keto, in turn, told the fact to her brother Satal during the celebration. One [[Realspace/Legends|space]] station that orbited the yellow-colored planet continued its operations under the Krath rule. [[3996 BBY|A year later]],According to [[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 1]], the [[Tetan carbonite smelter]] was [[Attack on a Tetan carbonite smelter|attacked]] by the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] shortly before they [[Duel on Kuar|allied]] themselves with [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]], which is dated to the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3996 BBY]] by ''[[The Essential Atlas]]''. the warriors known as the [[Mandalorian Crusaders]] sought a challenge and hoped to provoke a battle with the military forces of the Krath cult. To that end, the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] under the leadership of [[Mandalore the Indomitable]] [[Attack on a Tetan carbonite smelter|attacked]] the carbonite smelter. Approaching the space station on their [[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk war droid]] mounts, the warriors destroyed at least a couple of the laser cannon emplacements that were busy firing at the attackers. Eventually a pair of Mandalorians who were towing an [[atomic compression bomb]]—a particularly potent explosive device—released the bomb toward the station, with the weapon attaching itself to the station's hull and causing a large explosion that caused the carbonite smelter to crash on the planet's surface. The Mandalorian attack and the crash of the carbonite smelter was witnessed by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]], who was one of the leaders of the Krath cult at the [[Time/Legends|time]]. The attack prompted Qel-Droma to contact the Mandalorian [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|leader]] and demand [[Gender/Legends|his]] surrender. ==Behind the scenes== The Tetan carbonite smelter first appeared in the [[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 1|first]] issue of [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith|''Dark Lords of the Sith'']] story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'']] series of [[Star Wars comic books|comic books]]. The issue was published on [[October 1]], [[1994]] and was written by [[Tom Veitch]] and [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and penciled by [[Chris Gossett]]. ==Appearances== *[[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith'' audio drama]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 1|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 1]] {{1st}} *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 1]] {{1stID|Tetan carbonite smelter}} ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Tetan carbonite smelters}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Fundidora de carbonita }} [[Category:Carbon freezing technology]]
